query: Magnificent 7 tech companies (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) large acquisitions $10 billion or more announced or rumored in 2026, especially mid-2026. Any pending big M&A deals as of June-July 2026?
answer:
## Summary

**Bottom line: As of June–July 2026, there is no confirmed pending $10 billion-or-more acquisition by any Magnificent 7 company awaiting close.**

Key findings:

1. **The only 2026 Mag 7 mega-deal already closed.** Alphabet's **$32B all-cash purchase of Wiz** (cloud security) closed **March 11, 2026** — Alphabet's largest-ever acquisition. It cleared DOJ antitrust review in Nov 2025, so it is not a mid-2026 pending deal.

2. **Rumored / unconfirmed deals near or below the threshold:**
   - **Amazon–Globalstar (~$9B, unconfirmed, below $10B):** reported ~April 2026 for Project Kuiper; complicated by Apple's ~20% Globalstar stake.
   - **Nvidia–PC maker (DENIED):** an April 13, 2026 SemiAccurate rumor (Dell/HP speculation) that Nvidia publicly denied.
   - **Tesla–SpaceX merger:** analyst speculation (JPMorgan) after SpaceX's June 2026 IPO — not a confirmed transaction, and SpaceX isn't a Mag 7 member.

3. **Large Mag 7 outlays that are NOT acquisitions:** Meta's ~$3.5B minority stake in EssilorLuxottica; Nvidia's >$40B of AI-infrastructure equity bets; Microsoft's restructured ~27% OpenAI partnership.

4. **The 2026 mega-deal action is concentrated in non–Mag 7 players:** Netflix–Warner Bros. Discovery (~$83B), SpaceX–Anysphere/Cursor (~$60B, June 16), PIF/Silver Lake–EA (~$55B), Charter–Cox (~$34.5B), and Palo Alto Networks–CyberArk (~$25B, closed Feb 2026).

Full structured findings (key facts, data points, and sources) have been recorded. All data accessed 2026-07-11; rumored items are explicitly flagged and no forecasts/probabilities are assigned.

sources.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
sources.snippet: Microsoft-OpenAI partnership restructured April 27, 2026; Microsoft holds ~27% of OpenAI (not an acquisition).

key_facts: Alphabet's $32B all-cash acquisition of Wiz — the only $10B+ Mag 7 deal of 2026 — CLOSED on March 11, 2026 (announced March 2025); it is Alphabet's largest-ever acquisition and no longer pending.
As of June–July 2026, no Magnificent 7 company has a publicly confirmed pending acquisition of $10B or more awaiting close.

Amazon was reportedly (~April 2, 2026) considering a ~$9B acquisition of satellite firm Globalstar for Project Kuiper; unconfirmed, below the $10B threshold, and complicated by Apple's ~20% ($1.5B) stake in Globalstar.
A SemiAccurate rumor (April 13, 2026) that Nvidia was negotiating to buy a large PC-oriented company (Dell/HP speculation) was publicly DENIED by Nvidia; no confirmed target or value.

Speculation about a SpaceX-led Tesla merger revived after SpaceX's ~June 12, 2026 IPO (raised >$85B, ~$2.2T market cap vs Tesla ~$1.4T); this is analyst commentary (e.g., JPMorgan), not an announced deal, and SpaceX is not a Mag 7 member.
Meta's ~$3.5B stake (~3%) in EssilorLuxottica (announced ~July 2025) is a minority investment, not an acquisition, and below $10B.
Nvidia deployed >$40B in AI-infrastructure equity investments in 2026 (e.g., $5B Intel stake now worth >$25B) — minority bets, not takeovers.

The largest pending/announced 2026 tech deals are non–Mag-7: Netflix–Warner Bros. Discovery (~$83B), SpaceX–Anysphere/Cursor (~$60B, June 16 2026), PIF/Silver Lake–Electronic Arts (~$55B), Charter–Cox (~$34.5B), Palo Alto Networks–CyberArk (~$25B, closed Feb 11 2026).
Q1 2026 global M&A exceeded $1.2 trillion (LSEG); deal count down 17% YoY but total value up 26%, driven by larger AI-focused transactions.

data_points.metric: Nvidia cumulative AI equity investments in 2026
Netflix pending acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery
PIF/Silver Lake/Affinity take-private of Electronic Arts (pending)
Palo Alto Networks acquisition of CyberArk (closed)
